author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, Mark xiii. 32, ‘But of that day or that hour knoweth no one, not even the angels in heaven, neither the Son, but the Father.’

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Mark xiii. 32, ‘But of that day or that hour knoweth no one, not even the angels in heaven, neither the Son, but the Father.’

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The advice, Brethren, which ye have just heard Scripture give, when it tells us to watch for the last day, every one should think of as concerning his own last day; lest haply when ye judge or think the last day of the world to be far distant, ye slumber with respect to your own last day.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye have heard what Jesus said concerning the last day of this world, “That neither the Angels of heaven, nor the Son knew it, but the Father.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Where indeed there is a great difficulty, lest understanding this in a carnal way, we think that the Father knoweth anything which the Son knoweth not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For indeed when He said, “the Father knoweth it;” He said this because in the Father the Son also knoweth it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what is there in a day which was not made by the Word, by whom the day was made?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let no one then search out for the last Day, when it is to be; but let us watch all by our good lives, lest the last day of any one of us find us unprepared, and such as any one shall depart hence on his last day, such he be found in the last day of the world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nothing will then assist thee which thou shalt not have done here.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: His own works will succour, or his own works will overwhelm every one.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And how have we in the Psalm sung unto the Lord, “Lord, have mercy on me, for man hath trodden me down”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is called a man who lives after the manner of men.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it is said to them who live after God, “Ye are gods, and ye are all the children of the Most High.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But to the reprobate, who were called to be the sons of God, and who wished rather to be men, that is, to live after the manner of men, he says, “But ye shall die like men, and fall as one of the princes.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For that man is mortal, ought to avail for his instruction, not for boasting.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whereupon does a worm that is to die on the morrow boast himself?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I speak to your love, Brethren; proud mortals ought to be made blush by the devil.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he, though proud, is yet immortal; he is a spirit, though a malignant one.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The last day is kept in store for him at the end as his punishment; nevertheless he is not subject to the death to which we are subject.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But man heard the sentence, “Thou shalt surely die.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him make a good use of his punishment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is that I have said, “Let him make a good use of his punishment”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him not by that from which he received his punishment fall into pride; let him acknowledge that he is mortal, and let it break down his elation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him hear it said to him, “Why is earth and ashes proud?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Even if the devil is proud, he is not “earth and ashes.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore was it said, “But ye shall die like men, and shall fall as one of the princes.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye do not consider that ye are mortals, and ye are proud as the devil.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let man then make a good use of his punishment, Brethren; let him make a good use of his evil, that he may make advancement to his good.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who does not know, that the necessity of our dying is a punishment; and the more grievous, that we know not when?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The punishment is certain, the hour uncertain; and of that punishment alone are we certain in the ordinary course of human affairs.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All else of ours, both good and evil, is uncertain; death alone is certain.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is this that I say?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A child is conceived, perhaps it will be born, perhaps it will be an untimely birth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So it is uncertain: Perhaps he will grow up, perhaps he will not grow up; perhaps he will grow old, perhaps he will not grow old; perhaps he will be rich, perhaps poor; perhaps he will be distinguished, perhaps abased; perhaps he will have children, perhaps he will not; perhaps he will marry, perhaps not; and so on, whatever else among good things you may name.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now look too at the evils of life: Perhaps he will have sickness, perhaps he will have not; perhaps he will be stung by a serpent, perhaps not; perhap he will be devoured by a wild beast, perhaps he will not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so look at all evils; everywhere is there a “perhaps it will be,” and “perhaps it will not.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But canst thou say, “Perhaps he will die,” and “perhaps he will not die”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As when medical men examine an illness, and ascertain that it is fatal, they make this announcement; “He will die, he will not get over this.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So from the moment of a man’s birth, it may be said, “He will not get over this.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When he is born he begins to be ailing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When he dies, he ends indeed this ailment: but he knows not whether he does not fall into a worse.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The rich man in the Gospel had ended his voluptuous ailment, he came to a tormenting one.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the poor man ended his ailment, and arrived at perfect health.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But he made choice in this life of what he was to have hereafter; and what he reaped there, he sowed here.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore while we live we ought to watch, and to make choice of that which we may possess in the world to come.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us not love the world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It overwhelms its lovers, it conducts them to no good.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We must rather labour in it that it seduce us not, than fear lest it should fall.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, the world falleth; the Christian standeth firm; because Christ doth not fall.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For wherefore saith the Lord, “Rejoice, for that I have overcome the world”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We might answer Him if we pleased, “‘Rejoice,’ yes do Thou rejoice.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If Thou ‘hast overcome,’ do thou rejoice.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why should we?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why doth He say to us, “Rejoice;” but because it is for us that He hath overcome, for us hath fought?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For wherein fought He?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In that He took man’s nature upon Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Take away His birth of a virgin, take away that He emptied Himself, “taking the form of a servant, being made in the likeness of men, and found in fashion as a man;” take away this, and where is the combat, where the contest? where the trial? where the victory, which no battle has preceded?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All things were made by Him, and without Him was nothing made.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Could the Jews have crucified this Word?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Could those impious men have mocked this Word?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Could this Word have been buffeted?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Could this Word have been crowned with thorns?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But that He might suffer all this, “the Word was made flesh;” and after He had suffered all this, by rising again He “overcame.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then He hath “overcome” for us, to whom He hath shown the assurance of His resurrection.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou sayest then to God, “Have mercy upon me, O Lord, for man hath trodden me down.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do not “tread down” thyself, and man will not overcome thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For, lo, some powerful man alarms thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By what does he alarm thee?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I will spoil thee, will condemn, will torture, will kill thee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And thou criest, “Have mercy upon me, O Lord, for man hath trodden me down.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou say the truth, and mark thyself well, one dead “treads thee down,” because thou art afraid of the threats of a man; and man “treads thee down,” because thou wouldest not be afraid, unless thou wert a man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is the remedy then?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O man, cleave to God, by whom thou wast made a man; cleave fast to Him, put thy affiance in Him, call upon Him, let Him be thy strength.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Say to Him, “In Thee, O Lord, is my strength.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And then thou shalt sing at the threatenings of men; and what thou shalt sing hereafter, the Lord Himself telleth thee, “I will hope in God, I will not fear what man can do unto me.”
